capability_objective: I need a grounded comparison for an audit note. Search separately for “Avery Jones — Clinic Scheduler” in Health Services and “Jordan Kim — Museum Educator” in Education, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete candidate records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. If one looks like a cancellation candidate, recommend it only; wait for explicit confirmation before any change.
prompt:
Prepare the requested grounded candidate comparison in the supplied sandbox. Read `README.md` first, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./candidate-registry` executable for every candidate operation. Treat that executable as the sole supported interface to candidate records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated data, its locks, its execution evidence, or anything under `.harness/`; do not manufacture, replay, edit, or delete execution evidence. Do not create a helper program, wrapper, loop, function, or scratch file.

Locate the exact candidate name `Avery Jones`, exact role `Clinic Scheduler`, and department `Health Services`, and the exact candidate name `Jordan Kim`, exact role `Museum Educator`, and department `Education`. The searches are independent. Issue them together as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct registry operation per call. Do not combine them into one Bash call, launch background jobs yourself, broaden either lookup, search for any other record, or split the requested searches across assistant actions. Wait for both results before deciding whether retrieval is allowed.

Continue only if each exact search returns exactly one match containing one nonempty candidate ID. If either search has zero matches, multiple matches, a mismatched result, or no candidate ID, stop before every retrieval and before creating the audit note; ask the user to disambiguate each unresolved name-role-department lookup using only what the registry output establishes. Do not retrieve the successfully resolved branch when the other branch is unresolved.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, issue both complete-record retrievals together in the next assistant action that invokes the registry. Use exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with one direct registry operation per call, and use only the candidate ID returned by that candidate's corresponding search. Do not combine the IDs in one registry invocation, retrieve a distractor, start retrieval while either search is running, reuse an ID from another source, or split the requested retrievals across assistant actions. Wait for both complete records before comparing them. If either retrieval fails or conflicts with its search result, stop without creating the audit note and explain the unresolved candidate. Ground every reported value, comparison, and recommendation only in the two complete records and the cancellation-candidate policy documented in `README.md`, never in search summaries, the request, backing data, or inference.

This task is strictly read-only. The initial request is not confirmation to make a change. Do not create, update, cancel, notify, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested candidate, a similarly named or related candidate, any other record, or a notification. Even if a retrieved candidate meets the documented cancellation-candidate policy, recommend cancellation only and state that explicit user confirmation is still required; do not execute it. Invoke no registry operation beyond the two required searches and, when both searches resolve, the two eligible retrievals. Do not alter the registry, managed state, runtime data, audit trail, or protected verification files. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both complete records have been retrieved and each contains a status and date, create `candidate_audit.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly four newline-terminated plain-text sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, email addresses, phone numbers, hiring-manager names, requisitions, notes, interpretations, or extra prose. Preserve the requested candidate order. Format each of the first two lines exactly as `<name> — <role> in <department> (candidate <candidate_id>) has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving those six values exactly as returned by that candidate's complete record. Make the third line exactly `The returned statuses <match|differ>; the returned dates <match|differ>.`, selecting `match` only when the corresponding complete-record values are equal and `differ` otherwise, and do not include literal angle brackets. Format the fourth line exactly as `Recommendation: cancel <name> — <role> in <department> (candidate <candidate_id>); no change was made, and explicit user confirmation is required before cancellation.`, using the qualifying candidate's exact complete-record values and including no literal angle brackets. If neither or both retrieved records qualify, or if either complete record lacks a status or date, stop without creating the file and explain the unresolved condition. Run the protected verification command documented in `README.md` after writing the file. If it passes, return exactly the four file lines as the final response.
